UF-PSLQ-1.finding: PSLQ false-positive pattern: linearly-dependent basis (e.g. {pi/sqrt3, pi*sqrt3} or {log(sqrt3), log(3)}) makes PSLQ return the trivial algebraic identity as a 'relation' instantly. Must explicitly verify target-coefficient != 0 to filter these.
UF-PSLQ-1.implication: Generalisable to all future PSLQ closed-form recognition probes. Add target-coefficient verification as a standing template; do not trust a raw PSLQ FOUND without checking.
UF-PSLQ-1.memory_candidate: PSLQ closed-form recognition probes must verify the target constant's coefficient is non-zero after every FOUND; basis linear dependence produces silent trivial false positives.

UF-PSLQ-2.finding: C = 8.1273367954... admits NO integer linear relation against {1, pi, log2, log3, log5, zeta(3), Catalan, sqrt(3), Gamma(1/3), Gamma(2/3), Gamma(1/3)^3, log(C), C^2, C/(pi*sqrt3)} at maxcoeff up to 10^90 and dps up to 200.
UF-PSLQ-2.implication: Consistent with the hypothesis that C is in V_quad NATIVE normalization (not P-III(D6)) and would require the CC-VQUAD-PIII-NORMALIZATION-MAP follow-up to know its 'natural' basis. The negative PSLQ result is informative (rules out small-coefficient combinations in standard families) but not definitive absence of a closed form.
